metadata.purpose: Section-routing anchor words extracted directly from the 2026 AHA/ASA AIS Guideline text. Used in a two-stage routing pipeline: Stage 1 uses ALL terms (including global) for section-level co-occurrence scoring. Stage 2 drops global terms and uses only discriminating terms for content retrieval within the winning section.
metadata.usage: When a clinician asks a question, match anchor words in their query against this map to identify which section(s) to search. A query may match multiple sections.
metadata.source: 2026 Guideline for the Early Management of Patients With Acute Ischemic Stroke (AHA/ASA)

categories.trials: Named clinical trials referenced in the section

v1_1_changes: Added discrimination_tier to metadata explaining the 4-tier system
Restructured each section's anchor_words: each term is now {term, tier} instead of a bare string

discrimination_tiers.global: 5+ sections. Ubiquitous term (IVT, EVT, AIS, etc.). Participates in Stage 1 section routing via co-occurrence intersection. DROPPED in Stage 2 content retrieval.
discrimination_tiers.broad: 3-4 sections. Participates in Stage 1. In Stage 2: dropped if pinpoint/narrow terms already identify the section; kept if still needed to disambiguate.
discrimination_tiers.narrow: 2 sections. Strong routing signal. Participates in both Stage 1 and Stage 2.
discrimination_tiers.pinpoint: 1 section. Strongest signal. Directly identifies the target section. Participates in both stages.

routing_algorithm: 1. LLM parses query -> extracts anchor terms + intent.
3. Classify each matched term by tier (global/broad/narrow/pinpoint).
4. STAGE 1 - SECTION ROUTING: Score ALL sections using ALL matched terms.
Primary-role terms score 3x base weight. Mention-role terms score 1x.
Co-occurrence bonus: score *= (1 + 0.3 * (matched_terms - 1)) when 2+ terms match.
Highest-scoring section wins. If tie, use parsed intent as tiebreaker.
5. STAGE 2 - CONTENT RETRIEVAL: Drop global terms. If pinpoint/narrow exist, also drop broad.
Search within winning section using remaining discriminating terms.
Use intent_content_source_map.json to select content type (REC, SYN, RSS, KG, TBL, FIG).
6. If ALL terms are global -> primary/mention role breaks ties. If still tied, use intent.
v2_0_changes: Removed compound_anchors (replaced by two-stage scoring logic)
Added 'role' field (primary/mention) to all global and broad tier terms
Updated tier descriptions to specify Stage 1 vs Stage 2 behavior
